perturbation.original_text: i.any information concerning the Franchise, even if this information is known or available to the general public;
perturbation.changed_text: i.any information concerning the Franchise, only if this information is NOT known or available to the general public;
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of confidential information to exclude information known to the general public, it introduces a contradiction. Later clauses emphasize that the franchisee should refrain from disclosing any information even if it's known to the general public. This creates uncertainty about the scope of confidential information and what is protected. The in-text contradiction undermines the overall confidentiality commitment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: CONSIDERING THAT the potential Franchisee recognizes that the Franchisor is asking them to refrain from discussing the Franchise project and from disclosing any information (be it by written, oral, or electronic channels) to a third party (as described below), even if the information is known to the general public, since discussions between the potential Franchisee and a third party could jeopardize or be detrimental to the Franchisor’s interests;
perturbation.justification: The original text defined confidential information as including information known to the public. The changed text explicitly excludes information known to the public from the definition of confidential information. The contradicted text requires the franchisee to refrain from disclosing information even if it is known to the general public. This creates a direct conflict, as the changed text now implies that information known to the public is not confidential, contradicting the requirement to keep such information confidential.

perturbation.original_text: The potential Franchisee agrees to take the necessary measures to ensure that the provisions of the present Agreement are respected to the same or greater degree that they would reasonably expect the confidentiality of their own information of the same nature to be respected.
perturbation.changed_text: The potential Franchisee agrees to take the necessary measures to ensure that the provisions of the present Agreement are respected only if the Franchisor specifically requests in writing, but not beyond reasonable measures, to protect the Franchisor’s confidentiality.
perturbation.explanation: The original agreement enforces a degree of care equal to the potential Franchisee's own confidential information, but the revised text now introduces a condition where action is only triggered by a written request by the Franchisor. This weakens the franchisee's obligation. This means without a written request, the franchisee doesn't have to do anything, even if they see a breach occurring. This undermines the active protection of confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Non-disclosure: The potential Franchisee recognizes that any confidential information could be used to the detriment of the Franchisor and the Franchise, and that the Franchisee disclosing any confidential information could cause irreparable damage to the Franchisor and the Franchise. The potential Franchisee hereby agrees to respect unconditionally the confidential nature of all confidential information and to never disclose or communicate any confidential information to a third party, be it directly or indirectly, or knowingly use this information for any purpose whatsoever, except with the Franchisor’s written consent. Furthermore, the potential Franchisee agrees to not discuss or communicate any aspect whatsoever of the confidential information, be it directly or indirectly, to any agent, director, employee, or any other representative EXCEPT if these individuals (i) have been informed of the confidential nature of the information and (ii) have duly completed and signed the certificate presented in Annex “A”. The potential Franchisee will be held accountable for any violation of this Agreement committed by their directors, agents, employees, representatives, and other professional representatives.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a written request contingency for the Franchisee's responsibility to protect confidential information. This directly contradicts the 'Contradicted Text' which states the Franchisee 'agrees to respect unconditionally the confidential nature of all confidential information and to never disclose or communicate any confidential information,' and 'will be held accountable for any violation.' The changed text significantly weakens the Franchisee's responsibility, making it conditional, while the contradicted text makes it unconditional.

perturbation.original_text: Return of confidential information: All documents, letters, reports, protocols, notes, journals, drawings, plans, maps, sketches, spreadsheets, data, and any other written material, correspondence, archive, or e-mail (including any electronic material) containing confidential information, as well as any copies of these materials, must be returned by the potential Franchisee upon written request to do so from the Franchisor or at any time at the Franchisor’s discretion.
perturbation.changed_text: Return of confidential information: All documents, letters, reports, protocols, notes, journals, drawings, plans, maps, sketches, spreadsheets, data, and any other written material, correspondence, archive, or e-mail (including any electronic material) containing confidential information created before the date of execution of the agreement, as well as any copies of these materials, must be returned by the potential Franchisee upon written request to do so from the Franchisor or at any time at the Franchisor’s discretion.
perturbation.explanation: By specifying that ONLY confidential information created before the date of execution needs to be returned, it implies that information created AFTER that date does not need to be returned. This in-text contradiction limits the scope of the return obligation and creates uncertainty, in addition it weakens the Franchisor's control over the flow of confidential information, especially as it relates to confidential information generated during discussions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definitions: In this document, the following terms will have the following definitions: (a)“Third person” includes, but is not limited to, any person, company, association, organization, union, corporation, and any other entity or individual; (b)“Confidential information” refers to and encompasses any information acquired or received by the potential Franchisee from or concerning the Franchisor, including, without limiting the generality of the foregoing: i.any information concerning the Franchise, even if this information is known or available to the general public; ii.any information or data concerning the Franchise and the Franchisor; iii.any financial information concerning the Franchise and the Franchisor; iv.the present confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text limits the definition of 'confidential information' that needs to be returned to ONLY information created BEFORE the execution date of the agreement. The 'Contradicted Text' defines 'confidential information' much more broadly, encompassing 'any information acquired or received by the potential Franchisee from or concerning the Franchisor'. This includes information created after the agreement is executed. Therefore, the changed text creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted section, creating uncertainty about the scope of confidential information and whether information created after the execution date is excluded.
